title: A Slice of Fugazzeta Pizza on Avenida Corrientes in Buenos Aires, Argentina Province
description: Avenida Corrientes, Buenos Aires: A close-up, top-down perspective captures a single slice of traditional Argentine fugazzeta, resting prominently on a stark white ceramic plate. The composition utilizes a shallow depth of field, blurring the background to isolate the rich textures of melted cheese, vibrant tomato, and a generous heap of thinly sliced, sautéed onions piled high atop the dough. Harsh indoor overhead lighting casts subtle shadows, emphasizing the glistening, oily sheen of the onions and the charred edges of the crust. The setting reflects the no-frills, authentic atmosphere of a classic Buenos Aires pizzeria, where the focus remains entirely on the culinary craftsmanship rather than elaborate presentation. Subtle hints of oregano and dried herbs fleck the surface, hinting at the savory depth typical of local porteño flavors. The deep red tabletop provides a stark, dark contrast that draws the eye directly toward the warmth and golden hues of the pizza. This culinary snapshot highlights the enduring legacy of Italian immigration on the local gastronomy, which continues to shape the identity of the city's nightlife and dining culture. As one explores the iconic Avenida Corrientes, these humble, grease-laden slices serve as essential landmarks of the nocturnal urban experience. This image is a collaborative production by Samuel Jeffery and Audrey Bergner for Project 23.
